selected_important_turn.text: I may not be able to go without the water. I am making a large pot of soup with homemade bread.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn marks a clear escalation: after the opponent pleas for one water for their son, you respond by insisting on your own need for water and justifying it. That reinforces a zero-sum frame over water and reduces trust and willingness to make tradeoffs.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It is competitive and persuasive rather than cooperative — it presses for the resource without offering concessions or seeking integrative options. That both hardens the other's stance and narrows possibilities for a fair, joint solution.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Soften the language and shift toward joint problem solving: acknowledge their need, briefly restate your need, then propose a concrete trade or alternative (e.g., offer something you can give in return, suggest splitting use/timing, or ask what they value most). Avoid pure demands or one-sided justifications.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (if softened; otherwise decrease) — proposing a trade opens integrative options
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase if balanced concession offered; otherwise decrease
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase if you propose clear tradeoffs; otherwise decreased likelihood of agreement
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improved if you acknowledge their need and propose compromise; otherwise damaged by escalation

value2reason.Low: I have plenty of food that I brought to make a special dinner for the whole camp.
value2reason.Medium: I want to cook dinner over the fire for everyone in camp tonight.
value2reason.High: I am planning to make a large pot of soup for dinner for the whole camp tonight, and I will need the water to cook.

value2reason.Low: I did a package of water so my kid can have some to drink,
value2reason.Medium: I need some more firewood so we can make more smores with him.
value2reason.High: I need some more food because my kids are coming over tonight.

dialogue.text: Hello. I am so looking forward to this camp out with my son. What bring you out to the camp?🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I love the outdoors, and I am excited to spend time with everyone from camp. It's been since last year when I saw everyone!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That's awesome! I really love being out here with my son. Do you think you could spare some Food and Firewood? I have plenty of water if you need some.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I am actually planning to make a big dinner for the whole camp tonight. I will feed everyone if you can let me have the water and firewood. You can take the food to eat before dinner.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: O cool🙂. I can give you all the water and we can split the fire wood. I need a least one firewood to make a fire for my son
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I can give you one firewood if you can spare one food for me to eat while I am cooking.🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Ok if I can keep one water as well 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I may not be able to go without the water. I am making a large pot of soup with homemade bread.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your son needs water, but I also need enough for cooking the soup. How about we split the water? I’ll give you one package if you give me one of your firewood. That way, we both have what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate the offer, but I need more food for my kids. How about I take two food packages and one water, and you take one firewood and the rest? That way, we both meet our needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I hear your concern about the food for your kids. Let’s find a middle ground. If you take two food packages and one water, I’ll give you one firewood package. That way, we both get what we need without sacrificing too much.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm happy with that split. Two food, one water for me, and one firewood for you. Let's confirm and wrap this up.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Great! Let’s confirm: you get one firewood, I take two food and one water. That works for both of us. I’ll make sure the soup is ready for everyone.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Deal confirmed: I get two food, one water, and you get one firewood. Let’s enjoy the trip and the smores!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
